text.title: Rust Backend (Axum) Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real rust-backend deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: AppState held in a global Lazy<Mutex<_>>; tests cannot inject; production reaches for the singleton.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated AppState as a process-global.
antipattern.fix: Pass AppState via State<Arc<AppState>> extractor; tests build per-test instances.

antipattern.symptom: Heavy CPU work (hashing, image processing) inline in async handler; one request stalls all others on the worker.
antipattern.root-cause: Author missed spawn_blocking; treated all work as I/O.
antipattern.fix: Wrap CPU work in tokio::task::spawn_blocking; reserve async path for I/O.

antipattern.symptom: Model + db landed in PR1, handler + route in PR2; HEAD between has half-wired code.
antipattern.root-cause: Author committed by layer rather than by vertical slice.
antipattern.fix: Vertical-slice rule: model + db + service + handler + route + tests land in one PR per resource.
